title: ERM Persistent-State Schema (erm-persistent-state-v1.0)
description: Schema for the ERM (Environmental Risks & Planetary Boundaries) monitor's `persistent-state.json` — the canonical knowledge-base file the Applier writes to. The MVP shape is intentionally permissive on per-module structure (`additionalProperties: true` everywhere outside `_meta`); per-module strictness is the responsibility of future MBC sweeps. The strict layer is the `_meta` block — `schema_version`, `last_updated`, `last_issue`, `monitor_slug`. Sprint-5 Phase 3 (BRIEF cf14eabd §Phase 3) adds a top-level `arrays` block declaring, for every persistent-state array ERM cares about, the merge `key_field` and a `key_constraint` (regex pattern OR string enum). The engine reads these declarations to enforce additive merge semantics and raise `DisjointKeySchemeError` on key-scheme rotation (E10 cure).

schema_version.description: Schema version of this file. New canonical value: `erm-persistent-state-v1.0`. Legacy bake value `2.0` (string or number) accepted during the AD-2026-04-26Y bake window.

last_updated.description: Most recent material state change. ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) or full ISO-8601 timestamp. Bumped by the Applier on cycles that land at least one patch; NOT bumped on null cycles.

last_issue.description: Issue counter. Applier increments by 1 on cycles that land at least one patch when the current value is an integer.

policy_law_compliance.describe: Environmental policy, law, and compliance tracker. `jurisdiction` is the governing jurisdiction name (EU, USA, UK, India, etc.). Free-form pattern — jurisdiction naming conventions are not stable enough to enum-lock; new jurisdictions enter without a schema bump.

jurisdiction_risk_matrix.describe: Per-jurisdiction environmental risk matrix (framework readiness, enforcement capacity, climate exposure, overall risk). `jurisdiction` is the jurisdiction name — free-form to accommodate all relevant state and non-state actors. Forward-declared as a persistent-state candidate; interpreter-schema coverage gate exits clean regardless of whether the array is actively persisted.

key_judgments.describe: ERM key-judgments array. `id` follows the ERM-KJ-NNN scheme (e.g. 'ERM-KJ-001'). Pattern lock prevents the LLM from rotating to free-form or cross-monitor identifier variants mid-cycle — mirrors the AGM-KJ-NNN precedent (cb6fd959).
